accept: After this lands, a typical 'claim → work → close → push' loop produces exactly 2 git commits on main: one claim, one work-with-close-op.
Test asserts the count: file an issue, claim it, edit code, commit-and-close in one step, assert main has +2 commits and the second commit's tree contains both the code change and the close op file.
Forward-only: this design must not require rewriting existing v0.1 commits. Old per_session and per_op behavior continues to work for in-progress claims started before the upgrade.
Doctor orphan-close check passes on a repo that uses the new design.
act close --help documents the new flow and the relationship to git commit.
payload.description:
Forward fix for the commit-noise problem (act-728d's bundling didn't help our actual workflow because per_session bundles intermediate ops, but our typical lifecycle has no intermediate ops between claim and close — see post-bundling analysis 2026-05-10).

The ideal: 2 git commits per task lifecycle.
1. Claim commit (act-op only) — required for cross-agent visibility; deferring it means two agents can race on the same issue.
2. Work commit — contains the agent's code changes AND the close op file, with subject ending in (act-XXXX). The standalone act-op close commit goes away.

Implementation sketch:
- act close detects whether there's an unpushed pending work commit and either (a) writes the close op file, stages it, and amends the most recent commit (only if unpushed); or (b) writes the close op file and waits — agent runs git commit -am '<msg> (act-XXXX)' next, picking up the staged close op as part of that commit.
- (b) is safer — no amending — but requires a slight workflow shift: agents do 'act prepare-close' or just 'act close', then their next git commit subsumes both code changes and the close op.
- Either way: claim still commits immediately and pushes (cross-agent visibility preserved).
- doctor's orphan-close grep continues to work because work commits still embed (act-XXXX).
- Bundling for per_session can be deprecated or repurposed: with this design, the auto-bundle-on-close becomes equivalent or unnecessary.
payload.title: redesign: close op stages into work commit, eliminating standalone close commits
